Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real technique behind it. Marcus confirmed the cornmeal-jalapeño structure was legitimate. Stephanie proposed leading with the moisture-seal angle, which Margaret approved - Marcus will anchor copy around how the cornmeal crust concentrates heat on the bite.

Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should anchor on crust performance rather than ingredients. All three aligned on shipping.

Wednesday: Julian presented three bite-through shots. Stephanie pushed for shot two - the crisp exterior shattering - over Margaret's preferred jalapeño cross-section. Margaret agreed shot two works, saying "the crisp matters more than the cross-section." Decision locked: shot two leads.

Stephanie: "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?"

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focusing on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret pushed to lead with the steam-absorption mechanic - not taste. Stephanie said "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" then asked Marcus to cut repetition. Margaret approved the tightened version: show what happens when frying, skip over-explaining the science.

Friday: Margaret opened with "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - expecting problems. Marcus pushed back, saying trusting the work improved it. Stephanie credited the team for listening over defending positions. Marcus and Julian both agreed restraint - in copy and camera angles - was the deciding factor, not a compromise.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - no ingredients, techniques, or food ideas were mentioned beyond a passing analogy. Devon handled a technical deployment and confirmed it was ready to go live. Margaret favored a restrained approach, which Devon agreed with. No food-related decisions or disagreements occurred.
